response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with over 15 years of web, mobile, and embedded systems work, but his profile has essentially zero overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep hardware expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD workloads, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His performance engineering experience is limited to application-level low-latency work (async I/O, caching) and his recent AI exposure is at the infrastructure/tooling layer, not hardware optimization for deep-learning workloads.
strengths: Long career (15+ years) with demonstrated ability to work across the stack and adapt to new technologies quickly
Some relevant low-level performance experience: low-latency C/C++ and DLang work with async I/O and high-throughput systems at Sociomantic/Dunnhumby
Computer Science BS from MIT provides the foundational engineering education baseline the role requires
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — the primary technical requirement for this role is entirely absent
No background in deep-learning, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator workloads — all preferred qualifications are unmet
Principal-level hardware architecture roles require specialization in silicon and systems; this candidate's entire career is application and web-layer software

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning accelerator or GPU performance background
